observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002, or the MNGAS, Volume I, on the manner of recording the inventory accounts and recognition of corresponding expense accounts, by observing the following:
The Procurement Office to provide CGSO, copy of POs or contracts for the procured supplies, materials and goods immediately after perfection of the contract.  The POs shall serve as reference of the CGSO on the delivery terms and for the subsequent preparations of AIRs and recording of eventual inventories/ equipment in their records.
The CGSO to –
To coordinate with the Procurement Office and the CAO, on a periodic basis, on the status of POs or contracts’ issuance for the procured supplies, materials and goods;
Based on the POs provided by the Procurement Office, document the acceptance, inspection and recording of inventories/ equipment thru the use of the AIR completely filled up with the necessary information and complete documentation;
To require from end users and property officer/warehouse keeper the submission of Utilization Report for the procurement made and to document the SSMI and RIS; and
To submit to CAO, after the acceptance on the deliveries of the procured supplies, materials and goods, copies of POs, AIRs and supporting documents for the recognition of Inventory accounts in the books; and the monthly Report of SSMI supported with RIS and documentation, for the recording of inventory utilization.
The CAO to -
To coordinate with CGSO, on a periodic basis, on the status of AIRs completion supported with POs and other documents; and
To see to it that the recorded inventory accounts shall be supported with AIR, sales invoices with delivery receipts and PO reference; while the recorded utilization or issuance of supplies be documented with SSMI and RIS with complete documentation.

We recommended that the Information Technology Office (ITO) fast track, as part of the enhanced information system, the generation of a complete report for RPT delinquencies, to prove correctness of the reported receivables.
Thereafter, we recommended that the City Treasurer’s Office and City Accounting Office undertake periodic reconciliation of RPT/SET Receivables with the reported delinquent taxes, as basis for the subsequent collection of taxes.

We commended the Management in the increase of revenues over the last year’s figures. However, we still recommended Management thru the Local Finance Committee to:
Conduct periodic review of its collection efficiency to be assured of improved performance and higher revenues generated. Quarterly review is suggested in order to keep track of revenue accounts that register decline in performance efficiency.
Use the revenue targets in the real property taxes to further intensity collections therefrom. Avail of the administrative and judicial remedies in enforcing payments particularly in tax delinquencies.
Perform close monitoring of income generated from Real Property Taxes (RPT) - Basic, and Permit Fees and other decrease in income to ensure efficient collection.

We recommended that the City engage a service provider for the automation of a complete information system on real property administration particularly in assessment should an in-house development is not possible.  The amount of real property taxes that can be generated will far exceed the cost to be spent for the purpose. The complete inventory of real property units in the City will therefor initiate correct assessments and ultimately enable the intensification in the collection of RPT.

We recommended that the OBO closely coordinate the OCA regarding the timely submission of information on the building permits issued on account of new construction and renovation as required in Section 210 of RA No. 7160.  Other relevant facts should also be passed on to the City Assessor in order that reasonable assessment is made. This will ensure that correct and timely assessment is made and that accurate real property taxes are paid to the City.

We recommended that the City Treasurer’s Office prepare the presumptive income levels for the approval of the Sangguniang Panlungsod and widely disseminate to the taxpayers who own/operate business establishments. This will provide the assurance of its conformance with RA No. 7160 and BLGF No. 01-001 and at the same time allowing the taxpaying public to be aware of the system that the Treasury implements as regards local taxation.

We take note that Management is in the process of revising and formulating the Revenue Ordinance of the City on account of our previous audit recommendation taking note of the fact that the original Code was enacted in 1993 though series of amendments were undertaken through the years.  In view of above observations, we recommended the inclusion of the following in the formulation of the new Code:
Rationalize Section 9 to ascertain the places of amusement that charged admission fees and segregate these from amusement places that should report the gross receipts from the preceding calendar year.  We suggest further the amendment of the provision of the Ordinance so as to correctly enforce the imposition of Amusement Tax.
Amend Tax on Peddlers to conform with the provision of 
Sections 129 and 143(g) of RA No. 7160 since imposition of that from this source is a mandate of the City which could not be delegated.  This will assure that proper imposition and collection shall be correctly carried out and proceeds shall go to the proper account of the City.
Exclusion of owners of real property who are not persons engaged in the business of selling from those responsible to pay the Tax on Real Estate Sellers and Lessors.  This will assure the collection of correct amounts from taxpayers liable to pay the tax.
Limit Professional and Occupational Tax to Professional Tax only to conform with the provision of Section 139 of RA No. 7160.
Introduce or include amendment to the provision of the Ordinance on the tax to be imposed on newly started business of printers and publishers to conform to the requirements of Section 136 of 
RA No. 7160.  This will ensure that the imposition is in accord with the law and that generation is maximized from this revenue source.

We recommended that the requirement of Section 11(b)(1) of 
RA No. 10121 be observed by requiring the CDRRMO to render the Status of Implementation for DRRM-PPAs funded out of CYs 2020 to 2023 unexpended DRRMF, for proper monitoring.
We also recommended that the CAO comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, on the submission of monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Funds in addition to the quarterly report required by DILG and provide a copy to CDRRMO as basis of their monitoring and eventual preparation of the Status of Implementation of DRMM-PPAs for CYs 2020 to 2023.

We recommended strict observance of Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular 
No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, by formulating a separate LDRRMFIP for the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years including the balances of continuing appropriations in the GF. Such LDRRMFIP shall be documented with a list of specific projects and activities for implementation which shall show a yearly breakdown of unimplemented projects/activities.

We commended the Management for the high implementation rate of its planned programs, projects and activities for GAD and for prioritizing PPAs that will address City’s gender issues and concerns. However, we still recommend full implementation of planned programs, projects, and activities for GAD in compliance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended CENRO to actively monitor the execution of the planned project on the establishment of a Central MRF and Composting Center to comply with the provisions of RA No. 9003. This will effectively address the specific needs of the City and its constituent barangays, particularly in waste minimization and reduction.
We also commended the on-going efforts of the Management to consistently implement initiatives in collaboration with private organizations to promote the safe and sanitary management of solid waste generated in areas under the geographic and political coverage of Taguig.
We recommended that the Management continue to implement PPAs relative to its sustainable development goals.

We recommended and Management agreed to exhaust all available remedies provided under the laws, rules, and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance with the audit charges as prescribed by 
Section 7.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the CTO:

Stop the practice of issuing ORs for TCCs applied for payment of taxes as a deduction from their tax obligations; and

Support the Report of Collections and Deposits rendered by collectors with ORs, order of payments/tax bills, and copies of the TCCs.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented

Not Implemented
observations.reason:
Management is asserting that: Sections 67 and 68 of PD 1445 do not prohibit the issuance of official receipts upon payment of taxes through Tax Credit Certificates. In relations to this, Section 68 of the same Decree states that: “No payment of any nature shall be received by a collecting officer without immediately issuing an official receipt in acknowledgement thereof.”

Moreover, Section 21 of EO 266 allows tax credit certificate for the payment of taxes, duties, charges and fees due to the National Government.

Reports of Collection and Deposits submitted were not supported by the relevant documents such as the orders of payment, tax bills, and copy of TCCs, among others.

The CTO furnishes the CAO with the copies of all TCC issued.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that:
The Local Chief Executive, thru the City Administrator –
Instruct the created overall Inventory and Reconciliation Committee on PPE accounts to convene, undertake and complete the physical inventory of property, and conduct the reconciliation with the property and accounting records. The committee shall be required to render a report on completion and reconciliation of the PPE accounts within the prescribed timetable;
Require the Procurement Office, to provide the CGSO, upon delivery of the properties, with copies of the sales invoice, approved purchase order, distribution list of the PPE supporting the AIR, as their reference in the preparation of the PAR, Inventory Tag and completion of the RPCPPE;
The Property Custodian/Officer, the CGSO, and the CAO to –
Set up an effective system to keep track and capture the related transactions on PPE, reconcile and adjust PPE records regularly accordingly, and promptly reconcile the results of the physical count with the accountabilities recorded in the books of accounts and the property cards; and
Submit the reconciled RPCPPE to the Auditor within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that Management require:
The CTO to closely coordinate with the bank officials on the status of the requested copies of the bank’s credit and debit memos and other reconciling items for submission to the CAO to support the adjustments to the related asset and government equity accounts;
The CAO to –
Upon receipt of the documents from the CTO, prepare immediately the necessary entries for the reconciling items; and
Coordinate with the CTO for the immediate clearing of reconciling items pertaining to the difference of P6,408,293.60 between the bank and book beginning balance.
Management comments/actions:
Management assured that they will enjoin the CAO and CTO for the continuous coordination with the depository bank to secure copies of bank credit/debit memos and to further analyze the accounts and effect necessary adjustments to bring up the correct balances of the accounts.

We  recommended  that  the  CAO  and  CTO exert efforts to further analyze and determine the reasons for the non-movement of the three depository accounts in the books, unconfirmed one depository account and make the necessary adjustments to correct the book balances.
Management comment/action:
Management assured that they will enjoin the CAO and CTO to further analyze the accounts and effect necessary adjustments to bring up the correct balances of the accounts.

We recommended that Management require:
The CAO to -
Assign a personnel to handle the supervision and monitoring of financial accounts and analysis. The task shall preferably be handled by a Certified Public Accountant;
Consider the future inclusion/creation in the CAO of a specific division to handle the financial accounts and analysis, whose task is in addition to the bookkeeping function;
The CAO to –
Temporarily assign a personnel to analyze the Inventory accounts and coordinate with the CGSO and/or concerned Offices for the reconciliation of such account. Thereafter, require the submission of Report of Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) and other documentation from the Offices concerned, necessary for dropping the inventory accounts and to reduce the amounts for slow utilization movement of inventory account balance;
The General Services Office (GSO) to –
Regularly coordinate with the CAO on the status of inventory accounts. Subsequently require from end users and property officer/warehouse keeper the submission of Utilization Report/Distribution List and other documentation; and
Submit to CAO the SSMI supported with RIS and necessary documentation.
Management comment/action:
Management acceded to the audit recommendations and assured to closely work with CAO, GSO and concerned Offices in the reconciliation of the inventory accounts to comply with the submission of SSMI and RIS to CAO.

We reiterated our previous year audit recommendations that the Hospital Head of TPDH direct the in-charge of the PhilHealth Section to coordinate with the CAO on the status and reconciliation of the undistributed PF recorded under the Other Payables account of the City’s TF-TPDH, purposely to determine if the outstanding amounts pertains to PF or hospital fees. Thereafter, we recommended the necessary adjustments in the books.
For funds established as PF, we recommended to the Hospital Head of TPDH, the immediate distribution of the long outstanding amounts and the regular distribution of the fund preferably on a quarterly basis.
Management comment/action:
Management agreed to the recommendations and assured to implement the distribution of outstanding PF on a quarterly basis, once adjustments have been done.

We recommended Management to comply with the requirements of Section 86 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2006-002 dated January 31, 2006, through the undertaking of a Certificate showing appropriation to meet contract and ObR to form part of the procurement documents. The ObR issued for the appropriations to meet contract or as a pre-requisite of the procurement, shall have no specific name of the payee, only it shall be adjusted once, there is a definite name of payee or contract has been perfected/issued.
Management comment/action:
Management took note of the recommendations and assured compliance thereon.

We still recommended that the Management, thru the Local Finance Committee:
Consider a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue taking into account the prior years’ experience and statistics as benchmarks in setting targets;
Involve the CTO and other Departments in generating revenues to meet the targets;
Require the CTO to accomplish the Quarterly Report of Income to keep track of the status of revenue generation; and
Require the concerned officials to conduct a periodic review of the Quarterly Report of Income to ascertain whether collection targets are attained, to provide assurance of improved finances for the operations of the City.
Management comment/action:
Management took pride in exceeding its revenue projections despite the presence of the COVID-19 pandemic.  They took note of the recommendations and assured that thru the LFC, the conduct of periodic review of its collection efficiency shall be undertaken to further improve and intensify collections.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management consider the revision and codification of the City’s revenue rules and regulations within the prescribed timeline of implementation, in order to provide a ready reference for the implementers and the taxpayers.
We also recommended that Management coordinate with the Sangguniang Panlungsod to revisit the provisions of the CY 2017 Business Tax Ordinance to come up with amendments that would give clearer interpretation of revenue provisions.
Management comment/action:
Management informed that the process of codifying the Revenue Code, and the actions in complying with the recommendations were halted due to 
COVID-19 that hit the country in CY 2020 until now. They however assured to resume their actions towards assessment and codification, now that the pandemic seemed to stabilize.

We reiterated our recommendation that the CTO, the BPLO, and the evaluator or the person in-charge in the assessment of the tax due, as well as the approving Officer submit the necessary explanation on the occurrence of similar declared gross receipts in succeeding tax periods.
We also reiterated the recommendation that the CTO should consider exercising the mandate prescribed in Section 171 of RA No. 7160 to conduct an examination of the books of accounts of these business establishments to determine actual gross receipts for the periods when identical gross receipts and/or low or zero amount declaration in the current year are declared for the computation of the correct tax liabilities.
Thereafter, we recommended that the CTO examine and compute the actual tax liabilities of these business establishments, including penalties and charges, and the immediate demand for its collections.
Management comment/action:
Management informed that CTO assesses the local business tax based on the documents presented by taxpayers and the information available through records. CTO also informed that Letters of Authority were now issued for the examination of the books of accounts of business establishments to   ascertain the correct taxes, fees, and charges.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Management:
Initiate appropriate action against the service provider,  to address the generation of the following reports from the upgraded system, which may, among others, include:
Certified list of taxpayers with the corresponding amount due and collectible which serve as the basis of the City Accounting Office in the recording of RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year  in compliance with Section 20 of COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002, prescribing the use of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
The Tax Order of Payment/Tax Bill for business tax payments and Statement of Accounts (SOA) for RPT payments forming part of the Official Receipts for tax payments;
Monthly Abstract of Real Property Tax Collections with detailed information that would document the distribution and remittance of the RPT shares of the fund recipient.  The report is required pursuant to Section 52 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
RCDs summarizing the collections and deposits per satellite offices and summary RCD of the City Cashier; and
Soft copy of complete business tax payment records.
Ensure coordination between Offices of the City Assessor, City Treasurer, and City Accountant particularly on:
Acceptance of  the City Assessor  as process owner of data for the assessed value of real property linked/migrated to the upgraded  WBTISM revenue system;and
Acknowledgement of the City Accounting Office for the required Monthly Abstract of Real Property Tax Collections, with detailed information, per COA regulations.
Thereafter, submit regularly to COA Office thru the City Accounting Office the required reports mentioned in item A and all other requested reports to COA Office.
Management comment/action:
Management took note of the recommendations and assured compliance thereon.

We recommended that the Management to:
Formulate and setup policies or updating of  service standards for the processing of payment of claims;
Require the head of the department concerned, to adhere to the requirements of COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002, or MNGAS for LGUs Volume I, as follows:
Certification and approval as to validity, propriety, and legality of the claim signed by the head of the department or office concerned in Box A of the disbursement voucher.  In the absence of the department head’s signatory to Box A, the printed name and designation of the officer next-in-rank performing the function of the department head concerned and fully responsible for the Office should be indicated;
Maintain logbooks for incoming and outgoing disbursement vouchers to ensure proper, accurate and complete identification of incoming and outgoing documents and to ensure that timely delivery of the incoming and outgoing documents to its proper recipients; and
Include the involvement of the City Accountant as member of BAC Secretariat or in the Technical Working Group in order to address the prolonged time in complying with the documentary requirements, initially at the procurement level.
Management comments/actions:
Management assured that they will abide by the recommendations of COA and will formulate internal rules for payment.
Management informed further that they have been consistent in their drive to promote effective and efficient delivery of government services by prioritizing the health and safety of the citizens during pandemic which affected the detriment of the workforce. Due to the implementation of skeletal workforce as well as the fact that some of the employees were diagnosed with COVID-19, several projects for payments were delayed.

We recommended that compliance with the requirements of Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and Sections 118, 119, 116 and 121 of Vol. I of the MNGAS for LGUs, be observed as follows:

The Local Chief Executive, thru the City Administrator, –

Instruct the GSO, warehouse keepers and end users’ supply officers to require from suppliers upon delivery of procured items, the copies of delivery receipts bearing the SIs and the related PO reference;

Require the GSO to document acceptance, inspection and recording thru the use of the AIR properly filled up with the necessary information, and complete documentation as to PO reference, SIs/DRs reference and acceptance by the end user or property officer/warehouse keeper; and

Comply with the conduct of inspection of the procured items simultaneously with the acceptance of deliveries by the GSO.

Require the Chairman, the Committee of Inspection, and the members, who have the field of expertise, to sign the AIR for the delivered goods.

observations.recommendation:
1. We reiterated our recommendations that:


          i. The CTO coordinate with the bank officials on the status of the
             request for copies of bank credit and  debit  memos  and  other
             reconciling items for submission  to the  CAO  to  support  the
             adjustments;


         ii. The CAO –


            1.  Upon  receipt  of  the  documents  from  the  CTO,   prepare
               immediately the necessary entries for the reconciling  items;
               and


            2. Coordinate  with  the  CTO  for  the  immediate  clearing  of
               reconciling   items   pertaining   to   the   difference   of
               P6,408,293.60 between the bank and book beginning balance.

2. We recommended the following:


          i.  The Management –

            1. Create an overall Inventory and Reconciliation  Committee  on
               PPE accounts, tasked to undertake and complete  the  physical
               inventory  of  property  and  the  reconciliation  with   the
               property and accounting records. The committee to be  created
               shall preferably be a  composite  team  with  representatives
               from the CGSO, CAO, and the designated property custodian of
               the concerned department.  A  representative  from  the  City
               Engineer’s Office shall join the committee for the  inventory
               of Building and Other  Structures.  The  committee  shall  be
               required  to  render  a  report   on   the   completion   and
               reconciliation of the  PPE  accounts  within  the  prescribed
               timetable;

            2.  Assign  an  appraisal  committee  or  consider   hiring   an
               independent appraiser to establish the  reasonable  costs  or
               appraised values of  the  PPE  for  real  property  with  un-
               identified costs;

            3.  The  BAC  Secretariat  through  the  Office  of   the   City
               Administrator shall provide the CGSO  with  copies  of  sales
               invoice, approved purchase order, distribution list of the
               PPE supporting the AIR, as their reference in the preparation
               of PAR, Inventory Tag and completion of the RPCPPE;

         ii. The Property Custodian/Officer, CGSO and CAO –

            1. Set-up an effective system to  keep  track  and  capture  the
               related transactions on PPE; reconcile PPE records  regularly
               and adjust accordingly; and to promptly reconcile the results
               of the physical count with the accountabilities  recorded  in
               the books of accounts and the property cards; and

            2. Submit the  reconciled  RPCPPE  to  the  Auditor  within  the
               prescribed period mandated in the above COA Circulars.

3. We recommended that compliance with the  requirements  of  Section
   4(6) of PD 1445 and Sections 118, 119, 116 and  121  of  the  NGAS
   Manual, Vol. 1, be observed as follows:


          a. The Management to –


            1. Instruct the CGSO, warehouse keepers and  end  users’  supply
               officers to require from suppliers upon delivery of  procured
               items, the copies of delivery receipts bearing the Sales
               Invoices (SIs) number and the  related  Purchase  Order  (PO)
               reference;


            2. Require the CGSO to document the acceptance,  inspection  and
               recording thru the use of the AIR completely filled  up  with
               the necessary  information  and  complete  documentation  as
               to PO reference, SIs/DRs reference and Acceptance by the  end
               user or property officer/warehouse keeper;


            3.  Instruct  the  BAC   to   submit   the   delivery   receipts
               corresponding to the POs and sales invoice  attached  to   the
               financial transactions  to  support  the  recorded  Inventory
               Accounts;


          b. The CGSO to –


            4. Require the end user and property officer/warehouse keeper to
               separately prepare and sign an Acceptance  Report  with  POs,
               DRs and SIs reference, of the  delivered  goods,  which  will
               support the AIR signed by the CGSO Head;


            5. Submit to City Accounting  Office  (CAO),  the  monthly  SSMI
               supported  with  Requisition  Issue  Slip  (RIS)  with  a  duly
               acknowledged  List  of  Recipients/Distribution  List,  which
               shall also be indicated with PO/SIs/DRs reference,  as basis
               of the adjustment of Inventory accounts;

          c.    The Committee on Inspection to –


            6. Comply with the conduct of inspection of the  procured  items
               simultaneously with the acceptance of deliveries by the GSO;
            7.  Require  the  Chairman  and  members  of  the  Committee  on
               Inspection to sign the AIR for the delivered goods;


          d. The CAO to –


            8. To see to it that the recorded inventory  accounts  shall  be
               supported with AIRs, sales invoices  with  delivery  receipts
               and POs for reference;  while the recorded utilization or
               issuance of  supplies  be  documented  with  SSMIs  with  RIS
               together     with     duly     acknowledged      list      of
               recipients/distribution  list,  indicated  with   POs/SIs/DRs
               reference; and


            9. To see to it that the recorded delivered  or  procured  items
               and  utilization  shall  be  supported  with  the   documents
               required under items a and b, mentioned above.

4. We recommended that:


          a. The CTO –


            1. Stop the practice of issuing ORs for TCCs applied for payment  of
               taxes as a deduction from their tax obligations;


            2. Support the  Report  of  Collections  and  Deposits  rendered  by
               collectors with ORs, order of payments/tax bill and copies of the
               TCCs;

            3. Furnish and/or  inform  the  CAO  on  the  periodic  and  updated
               Schedule of taxpayers who have availed of  the  tax  credits and
               were issued with approved TCCs; and


          b. The CAO coordinate with the CTO in the periodic  submission  of
             the Schedule of taxpayers who have availed of the  tax  credits
             and were issued with approved TCCs and the supporting documents
             for the recording of appropriate accounts in the books.

5.6     We recommended that Management:


          i. Review the status of prior years’ payable accounts appearing as
             outstanding in the books; and


         ii. Undertake publication or confirmation of accounts prior  to  CY
             2018, and subsequently revert the long overdue payables to  the
             appropriate accounts of  the  General,  Special  Education  and
             Trust Funds, if determined that suppliers or contractors are no
             longer interested to claim the City’s unpaid obligations or  in
             case the payables are established to be undocumented.

5. We  recommended that :


          a.  The  Management  instruct  the  concerned  Department  on  the
             immediate implementation of programs/projects funded out of the
             dormant transferred funds.  Periodic monitoring on  the  status
             of the transferred funds should be undertaken to avoid slow/non-
              implementation of the projects/programs.

          b. The City Treasurer’s Office  coordinate with the  CAO  for  the
             remittance of the unused balance of P974,496.92 to  the  source
             agencies considering that  the  purpose  of  the  transfer  was
             already completed;


          c. The City Accounting Office –


            1.  Document  the  unutilized  transferred  funds  balances   of
               P974,496.92  for  purposes  of  remittance  to   the   source
               agencies; and


            2. Coordinate with the concerned department, like the Department
               of Health, for the subsequent action or implementation of the
               intended  programs/projects  based  on  the   Memorandum   of
               Agreements (MOAs) or other document defining the  purpose  of
               the transferred funds.

6. We  recommended  that  the  Management,  thru  the  Local  Finance
   Committee:


          a. Consider a systematic  and  realistic  forecasting  of  revenue
             taking into account the prior years’ experience and  statistics
             as aids in setting targets;


          b. Involve the CTO and other Departments in generating revenues to
             meet the targets;


          c. Require the CTO to accomplish the Quarterly Report of Income to
             keep track of the status of revenue generation; and


          d. Require the concerned officials to conduct periodic review  of
             the Quarterly Report of Income to ascertain whether collection
             targets are attained, to provide assurance of improved finances
             for the operations of the City.

7. We recommended that Management, thru the Local Finance Committee:


          a. Conduct quarterly review of its collection efficiency  to  keep
             track of revenue accounts that register decline in  performance
             efficiency;


          b. Use the assessed value as basis  of  revenue  targets  in  real
             property taxes  to  further  intensify  collections  therefrom.
             Avail of the administrative and judicial remedies in  enforcing
             payments particularly in tax delinquencies; and


          c.     Closely  monitorcollections  from  Receipts   from   Market
             Operations,  Tax  on  delivery  Trucks  and  Vans,  Share  from
             Economic Zone and other accounts that  registered  decrease  in
             income to ensure efficient collection.

8. We reiterated our  recommendation  that  Management  consider  the
   revision  and  codification  of  the  City’s  revenue  rules   and
   regulations  in  order  to  have  a  ready   reference   for   the
   implementers and the taxpayers.


       9.  We  also  recommended  that  Management   coordinate   with   the
          Sangguniang Panlungsod to revisit the provisions of  the  CY  2017
          Business Tax Ordinance to come up with amendments that would  give
          clearer interpretation of revenue provisions.

10. We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation  that  Management
          coordinate  with  the  Sangguniang  Panlungsod  to   revisit   the
          provisions of the Taguig Revenue Code particularly  for  SFMV  and
          other fees and charges to come up with amendments that redound  to
          the  best  interest  of  the  government  and  the  citizenry  and
          introduce new rates that are responsive to the current times.

11. We recommended that:


           a. The CTO should submit the DAS and TOP to support the reported
              collections, as proof of valid  information  on  the  selling
              price of the real properties and bases for computation of the
              tax due. Thereafter, require that all collections thereon  be
              properly and completely documented; and


           b.  The CAO should assure that official  receipts  in  support  of
              the reported transfer taxes be accompanied with copies of the
              DAS and TOP as bases in the booking-up of tax collections.

12. We recommended that the CTO  –


          i.  Propose  to  the  Sangguniang  Panlungsod  for  amendments  of
             Sections 24 of 2017 Revenue Ordinance, to include specific City
             Tax rates; and


         ii. Attach to the ORs the corresponding TOP.

13. We reiterated our recommendation that the  BAC  and  TWG  strictly
          observe the provisions of Sections 32 of the RIRR of RA  No.  9184
          in evaluating the entire procurement process and  undertaking  the
          post qualification activities necessary in determining  the basis
          in awarding the contract to the  contractor/supplier  who  offered
          the Lowest Calculated and Responsive Bid.

14.6   We recommended that Management:


          i. Comply with the provisions of the RIRR of RA No. 9184  and  COA
             Circular No. 2012-001, on the submission of the  documents  for
             infrastructure projects necessary for the  complete  review  of
             the City’s infrastructure contracts.


         ii. The members of the BAC and the TWG review, evaluate and  ensure
             the completeness of  the  documents  supporting  the  contracts
             entered into by the City to prove the integrity of the bidding
             process conducted.

14. We recommended that the City’s DRRM Council,  through  the  City’s
         DRRM  Officer,  formulate  and  implement  a   comprehensive   and
         integrated  Long  Term  LDRRM  Plan,  setting  down  the  expected
         outcomes, outputs, key activities, indicators and timeline under
         each of the four thematic areas, in alignment with the NDRRM  Plan
         as required in RA No. 10121.

15. We recommended the full implementation of the planned programs and
          projects of the LDRRM Funds necessary for the  attainment  of  the
          goals and objectives both of the City’s DRRM Plan and NDRRM Plan.

16. We recommended that the requirement of Section 11(b) (1) of RA No.
          10121 be observed by  requiring   the   CDRRMO   to   render   the
          Status  of  Implementation  for  DRRM-PPAs for proper monitoring.

17. We  recommended  full  implementation  of  the  planned  programs,
          projects and activities for GAD in compliance with the  provisions
          of JMC No. 2013-01.

19. We recommended that Management:


          i. Prepare and  design  an organizational  structure,  by  either
             merging or expanding the existing offices, to ensure  that  the
             staffing complement are aligned with the functions, performance
             targets and programs of the City;


         ii. Evaluate the qualifications of  consultants,  casual  employees
             and job order personnel to fill up the vacancies; and


        iii.  Consider   hiring   professionals   or   qualified   graduates
             particularly the beneficiaries of L.A.N.I. Scholarship  Program
             and TCU graduates, to join the regular work force of the City.

20. We recommended that the  Management  continue  to  implement  PPAs
          relative to its sustainable development goals.

21. We recommended that  Management  exhaust  all  available  remedies
          provided under  the  laws,  rules  and  regulations  in  enforcing
          immediate compliance of the audit charges as prescribed by Section
          7.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that: The CTO coordinate with the bank officials on the status of the requested copies of the bank’s credit and debit memos and other reconciling items for submission to the CAO to support the adjustments to the related asset and government equity accounts;
The CAO - Upon receipt of the documents from the CTO, prepare immediately the necessary entries for the reconciling items; and Coordinate with the CTO for the immediate clearing of reconciling items pertaining to the difference of P6,408,293.60 between the bank and book beginning balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The Management – Create an Inventory Committee on PPE accounts, tasked to undertake and complete the physical inventory of property and the reconciliation with the property and accounting records, and composed of representatives from the CGSO, CAO, City Engineer’s Office (CEO), and the designated property custodian of the concerned department. They are required to render a report on the completion and reconciliation of the PPE accounts within the prescribed timetable; Assign an appraisal committee to establish the reasonable costs or appraised values of the real property with unidentified costs; Instruct the BAC Secretariat to provide the CGSO with copies of POs, distribution list of the PPE supporting the AIR, as basis for the inventory costing and preparation/completion of RPCPPE; The Property Custodian/Officer, CGSO and CAO – Set-up an effective system to keep track and capture the related transactions on PPE; reconcile PPE records regularly and adjust accordingly; and to promptly reconcile the results of the physical count with the accountabilities recorded in the books and the property cards; Submit the reconciled RPCPPE to the Auditor within the prescribed period mandated in Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Designation of each Office Property Custodian was established. Adjustments in the amount of P44,852,303.84 was effected in the books during the year. Management considers this a work in progress. The proper Resolution for the creation of the committee is still to be determine. The selection of personnel to be assigned is currently undertaken. Management is partially implementing a system for PPE. RPCPPE were submitted only it lacked the costing of inventory in some of the accounts. The City Accountant and City General Services Officer (CGSO) analyze the discrepancies noted in the balances of PPE per physical count, SL/lapsing schedule to (i) effect the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts; (ii) prepare the Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards for all items with complete details; and (iii) adjust the balances of accumulated depreciations for all PPE accounts to present fairly the PPE balances in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The establishment of an inventory committee comprising of composite team from different offices, is still in the process of establishing the inventory committee. The retrieval of the remaining documents is still undertaken. The personnel assigned for the task is no longer connected with the City, hence, another work orientation is being undertaken. The suggested system is currently under study.

observations.recommendation: The City Treasurer – Settle inter-fund payables to the respective funds; Closely monitor the fund transfer to ensure that these transactions are properly and accurately recorded in the proper fund account; and The City Accountant coordinate with the City Treasurer on the periodic reconciliation of balances of the inter-fund receivables and payables to determine unsettled accounts and effect immediate settlement.
observations.managementAction: Inter-fund payables were subsequently settled. Fund transfers were closely monitored to ensure proper and accurate recording. Reconciliation of the accounts undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Finance Committee: Consider a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue taking into account prior years’ experience and statistics as aid in setting targets; and Conduct periodic review of collection performance to ascertain that collection targets are attained.
observations.managementAction: The City was able to meet its revenue projections for the year by P822.849 million, mainly due to collection of income from business taxes. Review has been made particularly in the 2020 budget deliberations.
observations.reason: Assessment is undertaken at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We commended Management, in particular the Business Permits and Licensing Office (BPLO) and the CTO, in surpassing the collections in Business Taxes over last year’s figures. However, we still recommended that Management thru the Local Finance Committee conduct periodic quarterly review of its collection efficiency to ensure improved performance and higher revenues generation.
observations.managementAction: The collections registered a net increase of P1.217 billion or 12.04 percent over the previous year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management consider the revision and codification of the City’s revenue rules and regulations in order to have a ready reference for the implementers and the taxpayers.
observations.managementAction: Management is currently in the process of revising the City’s revenue rules and regulations. The 2017 amendment of the City’s Business Tax is the start of the Management’s efforts to modify and codify all the City’s Tax Ordinances. Management assured that they will notify the COA Office for the updates.
observations.reason: Management is still consider this a work in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management coordinate with the Sangguniang Panlungsod to revisit the provisions of the Taguig Tax Revenue Code particularly on RPT and other fees and charges to come up with amendments and introduce new rates that would be responsive to the need of the City.
observations.managementAction: The Management is currently coordinating with the Sangguniang Panlungsod for possible amendments. The City notified that the market value will be coming from the Department of Finance for implementation of Local Government Units, but have yet to receive any circulars regarding this. Management also assured that they will work with the Sangguniang Panlungsod for the amendment of RPT rates. observations.reason: Management considers this a work in progress.

observations.recommendation: The City Treasurer and the Business Permit and Licensing Officer conduct inspection of the 5,146 business establishments and reconcile their records/lists. Thereafter, require the immediate imposition of business taxes, fees and charges and explanation from owners/proprietors on why they are operating without the benefit of business permits; and The City Treasury consider the enforcement of the requirement of Section 152 of the LGC where barangay clearances are to be submitted by business establishments before payment of business taxes and issuance of permits.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation was undertaken by Management. Recommendation was implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer and the BPLO provide the required information on the database particularly the gross receipts or sale for the preceding calendar year, taxes paid and the line of business, as basis in the proper computation of taxes.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Budget Officer and the Office of the City Mayor ensure that appropriations for Environmental/Sanitary Services, IT Services, Regulation and Enforcement, Social Services and Infrastructure are appropriately programmed in the budget as to the intended sectoral/functional classification and/or specific function/program/project. The purpose, object and specific codes of the expenditure provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015, should also be identified to correctly show the purpose of the expenditure to conform with the provisions of GAAM, Volume II and NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume III.
observations.managementAction: Management already effected the recommendation in the Annual Budget of 2020, during its deliberation in 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CEO, CBO, BAC and the Local School Board (LSB) together with the representative of the City Mayor’s Office review and evaluate the need-based requirement of the requisitioning Office which are translated in the PPMPs and APPs containing the requirements as mentioned in Section 7.3.2 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184. Henceforth, ensure that the succeeding PPMPs and APPs bear specific implementation schedule.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We commended the Management for the formulation of the current year’s CDRRM Plan. However, we recommended that Management, in the preparation of the succeeding year’s LDRRMFIP, consider the DRRM Plan as basis for the allocation of funds for the proposed projects and activities of the LDRRMF, to encompass the projects and activities programmed for the four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management activities required in the JMC and Section 12 (a) of RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CDRRM Council, through the CDRRM Officer, formulate and implement a comprehensive and integrated or Long Term DRRM Plan, setting down the expected outcomes, outputs, key activities, indicators and timeline under each of the four thematic areas, in alignment with the NDRRM Plan as required in RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: The long-term DRRM Plan is currently being drafted. Management informed that they will comply with the directive of RA No. 10121 on the preparation of Long Term DRRM Plan.
observations.reason: Management considers this a work in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRMO/LDRRMC, in the preparation and review of LDRRMFIP, consider the inclusion of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the Trust Fund, to help support the DRRM activities of the LDRRMC within the next five years.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (LDRRMO) ensure the optimal use of the LDRRM Funds by implementing the planned programs and activities under the four thematic areas for Mitigation Fund, which are necessary for the attainment of the goals and objectives both of the City’s LDRRM Plan and NDRRM Plan.
observations.managementAction: Efforts were made to comply with the recommendation. Management informed that during drafting of the LDRRM Plan, the City intended to use most of the LDRRMF in constructing drainage systems in the whole City. As such, almost 80% of the funds were allotted to the construction of the said infrastructure project.
observations.reason: Management considers this a work in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Office follow-up on the implementation of the CY 2018 planned project for the establishment of a Central MRF and Composting Center in compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9003, so that the needs of the City and its component barangays in addressing the waste minimization/reduction will be served.
observations.managementAction: MRFs were implemented by various barangays in answer to the requirements due to lack of space for the purpose.
observations.reason: Lack of space/land to build the Central MRF and considering the present economic development in the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City ensure that the planned programs, projects and activities for GAD be implemented in compliance with the provisions of JMC No. 2013-01 to benefit the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Continuous implementation of GAD PPAs. Management assured that they will effectively implement the planned programs, projects and activities for GAD in compliance with JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.reason: Assessment on the implementation of programs are undertaken at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Council initiate the establishment of GAD database as required in RA No. 9710 and JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: GAD database is already in place.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LSB: Support the lump-sum appropriations in the SEF Annual Budget with detailed listing of PPAs to be undertaken during the year; and Ensure proper planning by looking into the statistics relative to the needs of the schools, educators and the learning population.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LSB and BAC ensure that the APP/PPMP contain the complete list of items to be procured with details as to unit cost, quantity and period of implementation as basis in the procurement of the actual needs of the educators, target beneficiaries, schools and the learning population.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require: The City Treasurer to enforce legal remedies relative to the collection of delinquent taxes either by administrative action through levy on real property or by judicial action as prescribed under Section 256 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Income collected by SEF is sufficient to cover the appropriations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The Management create IAS unit, under the administrative supervision and control of the head of the agency to institute and conduct a program of internal audit for the agency, in compliance with Budget Circular No. 2004-4 to ensure that the government spends within means, invests in the right priorities, and delivers measurable results for greater transparency and accountability; The concerned City officials submit a proposal to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for the establishment of the Internal Audit Service/Unit in the City, including the organizational structure, functional charts, personnel complement, operating budget and funding source; and The Management adopt the Internal Audit Manual for Local Government Units as a guide in establishing, maintaining and operating an Internal Audit Service/Unit (IAS/U), pursuant to DBM Circular No. 110 dated June 10, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Initial steps were undertaken for the creation of IAS unit. The City is currently undertaking a study on this. The concerned City Officials submit a proposal to the Sangguniang Panlungsod. The City is currently undertaking a study on this.
observations.reason: The delayed implementation was due to difficulties in hiring/designation for a qualified personnel. The concerned City Officials has submitted proposal to the LCE. Management considers this a work in progress.

observations.recommendation: We strongly recommended that the concerned City officials comply with COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001 for the timely submission of the perfected contracts/POs for COA review and audit. Likewise, Management should ensure that the POs are duly approved and contain the basic information under existing rules and regulations as not to cause unnecessary delay in the audit of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation in 2020. The City informed that the Notices of Delivery (ND) and Acceptance and Inspection Reports were submitted on time. Management strongly recommended compliance with COA Circulars.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Officer/Inventory Committee prepare and submit the RPCI to support the year-end balance of P249.376 million, pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGU;

a. Consider a systematic  and  realistic  forecasting  of  revenue

We commended Management, in particular the  Business  Permits  and
Licensing Office (BPLO) and the CTO, in surpassing the collections
in Business Taxes over last year’s  figures.   However,  we  still
recommended that  Management  thru  the  Local  Finance  Committee
conduct periodic quarterly review of its collection efficiency  to
ensure improved performance and higher revenues generation.

We recommended that  Management  consider  the   revision   and
codification of the City’s revenue rules and regulations in  order
to have a ready reference for the implementers and the taxpayers.

We recommended that Management  coordinate  with  the  Sangguniang
Panlungsod to revisit the provisions of  the  Taguig  Tax  Revenue
Code particularly on RPT and other fees and  charges  to  come  up
with amendments and introduce new rates that would  be  responsive
to the need of the City.

a. The City  Treasurer  and  the  Business  Permit  and  Licensing
Officer conduct inspection of the 5,146 business establishments
and reconcile their  records/lists.   Thereafter,  require  the
immediate imposition of business taxes, fees  and  charges  and
explanation from owners/proprietors on why they  are  operating
without the benefit of business permits; and


          b. The City Treasury consider the enforcement of  the  requirement
             of Section 152 of the LGC where barangay clearances are  to  be
             submitted by business establishments before payment of business
             taxes and issuance of permits.

We recommended that the City Treasurer and the  BPLO  provide  the
required  information  on  the  database  particularly  the  gross
receipts or sale for the preceding calendar year, taxes  paid  and
the line of business, as basis in the proper computation of taxes.

We recommended that the Budget Officer and the Office of the  City
Mayor ensure that appropriations for Environmental/Sanitary Services,
IT  Services,  Regulation  and  Enforcement,  Social  Services  and
Infrastructure are appropriately programmed in the  budget  as  to  the
intended   sectoral/functional   classification    and/or    specific
function/program/project. The purpose, object and  specific  codes
of the expenditure provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009  dated
December 1, 2015, should also be identified to correctly show  the
purpose of the expenditure to conform with the provisions of GAAM,
Volume II and NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume III.

We recommended that the BAC  and  Technical  Working  Group  (TWG)
strictly observe the provisions of Sections 32 of the Revised  IRR
of
RA No. 9184 in  evaluating  the  entire  procurement  process  and
undertaking  the  post  qualification  activities   necessary   in
determining  the  basis  in   awarding   the   contract   to   the
contractor/supplier  who  offered  the   Lowest   Calculated   and
Responsive Bid.

We recommended that the City Engineer’s Office (CEO), City  Budget
Office (CBO), BAC and the Local School Board (LSB)  together  with
the representative of the City Mayor’s Office review and  evaluate
the need-based  requirement  of  the  requisitioning  Office  which
are translated in the PPMPs and APPs containing  the  requirements
as  mentioned  in  Section  7.3.2  of  the  RIRR  of  RA   No.   9184.
Henceforth,  ensure  that  the   succeeding   PPMPs   and   APPs
bear specific implementation schedule.

We commended the Management for the  formulation  of  the  current
year’s CDRRM  Plan.  However,  we  recommended  that  Management,   in
the preparation of the succeeding year’s LDRRMFIP, consider  the  DRRM
Plan as basis  for  the  allocation  of  funds  for  the  proposed
projects and activities of the LDRRMF, to encompass  the  projects
and activities programmed for the four thematic areas of  disaster
risk reduction and management activities required in the  JMC  and
Section 12 (a) of RA No. 10121.

We recommended that the CDRRM Council, through the CDRRM  Officer,
formulate and implement a comprehensive  and  integrated  or  Long
Term DRRM Plan, setting down the expected outcomes,  outputs,  key
activities,  indicators  and  timeline  under  each  of  the  four
thematic areas, in alignment with the NDRRM Plan as required in RA
No. 10121.

We recommended that the  LDRRMO/LDRRMC,  in  the  preparation  and
review  of  LDRRMFIP,  consider  the  inclusion  of  projects  and
activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE  of
the previous years which were transferred to the  Trust  Fund,  to
help support the DRRM activities of the  LDRRMC  within  the  next
five years.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk  Reduction  Management
Office (LDRRMO) ensure the optimal  use  of  the  LDRRM  Funds  by
implementing the planned programs and activities  under  the  four
thematic areas for Mitigation Fund, which are  necessary  for  the
attainment of the goals and objectives both of  the  City’s  LDRRM
Plan and NDRRM Plan.

We recommended that the Solid Waste Management  Office follow-up  on
the  implementation  of  the  CY  2018  planned  project  for  the
establishment  of  a  Central   MRF   and   Composting   Center    in
compliance  with  the  provisions  of  RA No. 9003, so  that  the
needs of the  City  and   its   component   barangays   in   addressing
the  waste  minimization/reduction will be served.

We recommended that the City ensure  that  the  planned  programs,
projects and activities for GAD be implemented in compliance  with
the provisions of JMC No. 2013-01 to benefit the constituents.

We recommended that the GAD Council initiate the establishment  of
GAD database as required in RA No. 9710 and JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that Management: Strictly comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992 which requires that only idle funds in the General Fund can be deposited under time deposit accounts upon prior authority of the Sanggunian and the approval of the local chief executive; Stop the practice of investing Special Education Fund and Trust Fund under time deposit accounts as these funds have specific purposes and do not fall within the context of idle fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 93-382; Unload the excessive investment in time deposits to expedite the settlement/remittance of amounts due to other government agencies and respective barangays including interests therefrom and to settle all financial obligations to creditors within due date; Distribute proportionately the interests earned from the time deposits under the Trust Fund among the sources/fund owners in compliance with Section 311 of RA 7160; and Adopt, by resolution, a comprehensive written investment policy to establish a set of basic procedures to meet investment objectives and to establish/maintain internal controls for accurate reporting and evaluation of investment results.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: Create the DRRMO which is mandated by law to be responsible for the development of plans and programs, oversee the implementation and undertake coordinated efforts on disaster risk management programs and activities of the city; Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from current year’s appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balance so that programs, projects and activities related to disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness program will be implemented within the scheduled timeframes. In such manner, the in-house capability to efficiently manage all types of emergencies and to achieve orderly transitions from response to sustained recovery in case of disaster/calamity will be strengthened to improve the disaster resilience of communities in the City; and Advise the concerned officials of the City to prepare the LDRMMIF indicating the list of priority projects and activities for the unexpended balance of LDRRMF for prior years. In such manner, the optimum amount of LDRRMF will be used efficiently and effectively for the intended purpose.

In such manner, the City Accountant may in turn submit the transaction documents to the Office of the Auditor within the period prescribed under Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Direct the City Accountant to diligently perform his duties and responsibilities; keep monitoring and overseeing the progress of reviewing, recording and submission of transaction documents to the Office of the City Auditor and strengthen the internal control system in the City;
Compel the City Accountant and his staff to expedite the recording in the books of accounts and the preparation of trial balances and financial statements for the City and Barangay accounts, in order to submit the same to the Office of the City Auditor in compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations; and
Ensures that the City Accountant follow strictly the laws, rules and regulations on the submission of accounts, otherwise, institute legal actions, administrative and/or criminal, for non-rendition of accounts within the prescribed deadlines.

We recommended that:
The City Accountant serve a final notice and demand to all concerned accountable officers to settle and liquidate their outstanding cash advances either by returning the money advanced if unspent, or by the presentation of liquidation documents. The transfer, separation or retirement of the accountable officer does not operate to discharge the person from his/her duty to account for the unliquidated cash advances; and
The City Mayor, who is bound by law to safeguard government fund against loss or wastage through illegal or improper disposition, cause the suspension of salaries of erring accountable officers and to undertake appropriate legal sanctions.

We recommended that Management:

Designate/appoint a  permanent DRRM Officer who is mandated by law to be responsible for the development of plans and programs, oversee the implementation and undertake coordinated efforts on disaster risk management programs and activities of the City;
Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from current year’s appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balance so that PPAs related to disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness program will be implemented. In such manner, the in-house capability to efficiently manage all types of emergencies and to achieve orderly transition from response to sustained recovery in case of disaster/calamity will be strengthened to improve the disaster resilience of communities in the City; and
Prepare the current LDRRMF budget indicating the list of priority projects and activities for the prior year unexpended balance of LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management:

Ensure proper identification of PPAs in accordance with  Republic Act No.10155; implementation of PPAs within the target period; and closely monitor their progress so that the intended beneficiaries may avail of public services that will address  the identified gender issues and concerns within the City.
Strictly comply with the requirement of Section  28  of  Republic Act No.10155, requiring the utilization of GAD  funds  equivalent to 5% of the budget; and
Revisit the payment scheme for scholars’ allowances and  consider the option of payment of scholars’ allowances  through Automated Teller Machine (ATM) or thru checks for  the  protection  of  the student-beneficiary.  In such manner, the grant of cash  advances to accountable  officers  in  big  amount  and  holding  of  cash advances in excess of authorized maximum accountability  will  be  stopped immediately.

We recommended that Management identify the specific PPAs to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund to ensure that detailed allocation for each project and relevant information are reflected in the AIP that will enable monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of the City’s development projects pursuant to DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2011-1.

We recommended that:
Management review and evaluate the requirements of requisitioning offices and take into consideration the timeliness of the procurement and delivery so as to avail the needed goods within the budget year to benefit the intended beneficiaries as planned;
The City General Services Office set up an effective and efficient procurement system to address noted weaknesses; and
All concerned offices/departments make as part of their regular process the immediate submission of copies of Acceptance and Inspection Report, sales invoices/billings to the City Accounting Office for proper recording of assets/expenses and the outstanding liabilities of the City.

We recommended that:
The City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to deputize bonded Barangay Treasurers to withhold the City’s tax from the contractors/suppliers dealings with their Barangay; and
The OIC-BPLO review/verify the declared gross receipts of contractors/suppliers doing business with the City barangays to ascertain correctness of declared gross sales/receipts as bases for computation of business taxes.

We recommended that Management require:
The Division/City/District Superintendent to prepare  the budget for the School Board supported by specific PPAs for the ensuing fiscal year as required in Sections 100(b) and 272 of RA No. 7160;
The concerned City Officials to validate the SEF budget for consistency with the AIP and APP as required in Article 410 of the IRR of RA No.7160; and
The concerned City Officials to adhere strictly with the provision of Section 7.2 of the IRR of RA No. 9184, mandating that all government procurement shall be in accordance with the APP, consistent with its duly approved budget.

We recommended that Management require:
The Local Finance Committee to make reasonable projection of collectible revenues, particularly real property tax, which is the basis of the annual budget for the SEF, in accordance with Section 235 of RA No. 7160; and
The City Treasurer to enforce legal remedies relative to the collection of delinquent taxes either by administrative action through levy on real property or by judicial action as prescribed under Section 256 of RA 7160.

We recommended that:
The City Mayor ensure strict compliance with the Full Disclosure Policy under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-083, as amended, by requiring the concerned city officials to post on time the required financial and operation reports in the City’s Official Website, DILG - FDP Portal Website, and in atleast three (3) publicly accessible and conspicuous places within the City. Further, ensure  that all the  information detail to the level of particulars as required in the said Memorandum Circular are fully disclosed in order to promote transparency, accountability and people’s awareness on the utilization of the city funds;
The City Treasurer, City Accountant, City Budget Officer, CGSO and other concerned City officials collaborate for the timely preparation and submission of required documents in order to comply with the period of posting prescribed under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2013-140; and
The City official/personnel in charge of posting in the City Official Website and DILG-PDP Portal Website immediately undertake appropriate actions to update all the postings in the two websites.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Management consider the codification of the revenue rules and regulations of the City to provide a clearer reading and ready reference to taxpayers and implementers. We further recommended that Management update the existing tax rates to be able to sustain an adequate, appropriate and relevant tax rates in relation to the City’s fast-paced growth and development requirements.

We recommended that:
The Management create IAS unit, under the administrative supervision and control of the City Mayor to institute and conduct a program of internal audit for the agency, in compliance with Budget Circular No. 2004-4 to ensure that the government spends within means, invests in the right priorities, and delivers measurable results for greater transparency and accountability;
The concerned City officials submit a proposal to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for the establishment of the Internal Audit Service/Unit in the City, including the organizational structure, functional charts, personnel complement, operating budget and funding source; and
The Management adopt the Internal Audit Manual for Local Government Units as a guide in establishing, maintaining and operating an Internal Audit Service/Unit (IAS/U), pursuant to DBM Circular No. 110 dated June 10, 2016.

We strongly recommended that the concerned City officials comply with COA Circular Nos. 96-010, 2009-001 and 95-206 for the timely submission of the perfected contracts/POs for COA review and audit. Likewise, Management ensure that the POs are duly approved and contain the basic information under existing rules and regulations as not to cause unnecessary delay in the audit of transactions.
We recommended that the City remit the full amount of unremitted balances as of December 31,2017.

We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies provided under the laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance of the audit suspensions and charges as prescribed by Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that: All concerned offices exert extensive efforts in retrieving the unrecorded/missing supporting documents for immediate submission to the City Accounting Department to effect the necessary adjustment to related asset and government equity accounts.
Henceforth, all concerned officials shall exercise the diligence of a good father of a family in safekeeping of public documents, recording and approving of transactions pursuant to Sections 122 and 127 of PD 1445. Failure to do so is subject to penal provisions under Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code; The City Treasurer coordinate with the bank officials (i) on the status of the requested copies of the bank’s debit and credit memos to support the necessary adjustments; (ii) to request bank statements and to update records pertaining to LBP Account No. 1822-1029-52 Logo Find for BRS preparation; and (iii) to obtain copies of bank records covering the four closed account; The City Treasurer and the City Accountant conduct quarterly reconciliation of the subsidiary ledgers for Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account and Treasurer’s cashbook balances in compliance with Section 74 of PD1445 to ensure the correctness and completeness of the recorded entries; The City Treasurer send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check at least one month before a check becomes stale; and Management impose appropriate actions provided under Sections 122 and 127 of PD 1445 against liable officials for the unjustified failure to submit the necessary supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Solid Waste Management Board: Adopt measures to promote and ensure the viability and effective implementation of solid waste management programs in its component barangays; Monitor the implementation of the City Solid Waste Management Plan through its various political subdivisions and in cooperation with the private sector and the NGOs; Review every two (2) years or as the need arises the City Solid Waste Management Plan for purposes of ensuring its sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the field of solid waste management; Develop the specific mechanics and guidelines for the implementation of the City Solid Waste Management Plan; Recommend to the appropriate local government authorities specific measures or proposals for franchise or build-operate-transfer agreements with duly recognized institutions pursuant to RA 6957, to provide either exclusive or non-exclusive authority for the collection, transfer, storage, processing, recycling or disposal of municipal solid waste. The proposals shall take into consideration appropriate government rules and regulations on contracts, franchise and build-operate-transfer agreements; Recommend measures and safeguards against pollution and for the preservation of the natural ecosystem; and Coordinate the efforts of its component barangays in the implementation of the City Solid Waste Management Plan.
